calendar.matters:
Hearing Set To Determine If Public Storage'S Sale Of Ms. Cornejo'S Property Is In Violation Of This Court'S July 18, 2018 Judgment Qualifies As Contempt.
Off Calendar Sep-17-2018 Continued To Oct-01-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 303

Hearing Set To Determine If Public Storage'S Sale Of Ms. Cornejo'S Property Is In Violation Of This Court'S July 18, 2018 Judgment Qualifies As Contempt.
Off Calendar Oct-01-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stephanie Malla, of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith, Tel. 415-438-6670, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Amador Brenneman, representing Public Storage #20280, Tel. 415-362-2580, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Marimar Cornejo, self-represented litigant, for defendants.

This case comes on calendar regularly for hearing to Determine if Public Storage's Sale of Ms. Cornejo's Property is in Violation of this Court's July 18, 2018, Judgment Qualifies As Contempt.

Ruling: The matter is discussed. After listening to testimony from both parties, the Court does not find Public Storage in contempt or violation due to ambiguity. The Court vacates Judge Kahn's order re providing the buyer's contact information. The Court can not provide the buyers contact information due to right to privacy of 3rd party, (Nist vs. Hall). Defendant must bring a motion or provide notice of hearing to request 3rd party contact information. Judge: Hon. Teri Jackson; Clerk: Felicia Green; JAVS Recording; Dept. 303.

Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Sep-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Eugene Lee, Esq. appearing by telephone for Plaintiff Public Storage;
Marimar Cornejo, Defendant and self-represented litigant, 415.767.6676, appears in pro per.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. Matter called.

Mr. Lee presented an update with regard to Ms Cornejo's property as follows: the property was sold for $400 - $600 dollars, the property included furniture/electronics/power tools/growing lamps, that Ms. Cornejo's property was sold at flea markets, that all of the property was sold, and that the buyer did not have detailed recordkeeping with regard to the sale of Ms. Cornejo's property. Mr Lee also stated that he was trying to get a declaration to support the information provided to the Court today. Mr. Lee addressed serious concerns with regard to recent events and his and his client's safety. Mr. Lee's concern was extended to him disclosing the buyer's name and contact information which he suggested be done in camera. Discussions were held.

Ms. Cornejo stated that her property was sold without there being a right to do so and that Plaintiff was in violation of the Order to Show Cause. Ms. Cornejo addressed her prior request for a bond and conversion. Additional discussions were held with regard to Ms. Cornejo's partner. The Court stated that it could not get into collateral/criminal cases.

The Court stated that it would direct Mr. Lee to disclose the name and contact information of the buyer to Ms. Cornejo ONLY if, under oath, Ms. Cornejo agrees to not disclose the information to any other person. The Court stated that Ms. Cornejo needed to understand that this would be the Court's order and that a violation of this order would be contempt of court. The oath was administered by the clerk. Ms. Cornejo affirmed. At the request of the Court, Department 302 Staff Attorney Patrick Burton will assist Ms. Cornejo in contactng the buyer in an attempt to reclaim her property.

The Court noted that if Ms. Cornejo can't get all of her property back that she could seek legal counsel before proceeding, that it could not give her legal advice, and that this Court's focus had been on restoring her property.

Mr. Lee was ordered to prepare a form of order and submit it to Department 302. (Please see the Official Court Reporter transcript for complete details of the hearing and the Court's order.)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Sep-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Marimar Cornejo, Defendant and self-represented litigant, 415.767.6676, appears in pro per;
Public Storage representatives were not present in open court at the time that this matter was called.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. Matter called.

At the request of the Court, Patrick Burton, Staff Attorney for Department 302, provides an update with regard to the current whereabouts of Ms. Cornejo's property as provided to him by a representative for the Plaintiff Public Storage. Mr. Burton stated that he was told that the property was sold for $25.00 to a buyer, that the buyer sold some of the property at a flea market, and that the buyer still has some of the property. Mr. Burton further stated that the buyer could provide an inventory by way of a declaration. At the conclusion of Mr. Burton's update, the Court continued the matter to September 7, 2018 at 11:00 a.m. in Department 302. The matter will be heard by Judge Kahn. Counsel for Public Storage/Plaintiff Public Storage's representative may appear by telephone for the hearing on September 7, 2018.

No ruling on the Order to Show Cause was issued by the Court today except with regard to Ms. Cornejo's property. The Court ordered an inventory of Ms. Cornejo's property and ordered that none of the remaining property be sold.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Eugene S. Lee, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois, 415.438.5928, appearing for Plaintiff Public Storage;
Amador Brenneman, represenatative for Public Storage; appearing with counsel for Plaintiff;
Marimar Cornejo, self-represented litigant, 415.967.6676, pro per Defendant also appeared.

Matter called. The Court asked Mr. Lee if he could provide the information with regard to the person in possession of Ms. Cornejo's property. Mr. Lee stated that he did not have that information in court with him today. Mr. Brenneman stated that the property was sold to one of their bidders and that he could obtain the information. The Court and the parties reviewed the July 18, 2018 judgment in open court. Discussions are held.

At the conclusion of discussions, the Court continued the Order To Show Cause to September 6, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. Counsel for Public Storage is to take all reasonable and extraordinary steps to obtain the requested information. Ms. Cornejo's request that Plaintiff post an undertaking/bond in the amount of $500,000.00 was denied by the Court as premature.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Continued To Jul-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Marimar Cornejo for appellant/defendants;
Amador Brenneman, of Public Storage #20280, for respondents/plaintiffs.

This case comes on calendar regularly for small claims trial de novo. All parties and witnesses are sworn.

Marimar Cornejo testifies on behalf appellants/defendants.

Amador Brenneman, testifies on behalf of Public Storage #20280, for respondents/plaintiffs.

The Court finds that UCC 1308 does not apply to this situation and there is no explicit reservation of rights.

At the conclusion of the hearing, the Small Claims Judgment is affirmed. The small claims judgment remains the same. Judgment is ordered in favor of Public Storage #20280, and against Marimar Cornejo, in the amount of $1932., principal, $65., for costs., and $195.20 for interest, for a total judgment of: $2212.20. Public Storage #20280 shall return all of Marimar Cornejo's property contained in the unit upon payment in full of this judgment. Public Storage #20280 is authorized to sell the said property if Marimar Cornejo or representative does not pay the judgment within 45 days of receipt of judgment.

Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 303.
